name: Vía Verde del Tajuña – from Perales de Tajuña to Ambite
name_original: Vía Verde del Tajuña – de Perales de Tajuña a Ambite
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Perales De Tajuña traverse the Tajuña Valley, characterized by agricultural landscapes, olive groves, and orchards. The region features the Tajuña Greenway, a converted railway line offering accessible paths. The terrain includes smooth profiles along the river and unique gypsum outcrops. This area provides a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Perales De Tajuña The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde del Tajuña – from Perales de Tajuña to Ambite, a 28.5 miles (45.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Tajuña Greenway through the valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Morata de Tajuña Town Square – Tajuña Greenway loop from Tielmes, a moderate 20.6 miles (33.2 km) path. This route loops through agricultural landscapes and along sections of the Tajuña Greenway. Local touring cyclists also love the Chinchón – Morata de Tajuña Town Square loop from Tielmes, a 35.5 miles (57.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain connecting towns, often completed in about 4 hours 39 minutes. Touring cycling around Perales De Tajuña is defined by the Tajuña Greenway, agricultural landscapes, and riverside areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Perales De Tajuña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Perales De Tajuña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Perales De Tajuña?
A: There are over 170 touring cycling routes in the Perales De Tajuña area, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Perales De Tajuña is diverse. You'll primarily find smooth profiles along the Tajuña River and agricultural landscapes with olive groves and orchards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961433080/via-verde-del-tajuna-from-perales-de-tajuna-to-ambite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Tajuña&lt;/a&gt;, a converted railway line, offers accessible paved paths, often with a distinctive red asphalt surface. While many sections are relatively flat, some routes include moderate ascents and descents, and you might encounter unique geological features like gypsum outcrops.

Q: Are there family-friendly cycling routes in Perales De Tajuña?
A: Yes, the region is well-suited for family cycling, especially along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961433080/via-verde-del-tajuna-from-perales-de-tajuna-to-ambite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Tajuña&lt;/a&gt;. This path is largely closed to traffic and provides rest areas, making it safe and enjoyable for various fitness levels. There are 12 easy routes available, perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: As you cycle, you can enjoy the authentic rural territory of the Tajuña Valley, with its agricultural landscapes, olive groves, and cornfields. The routes often follow the Tajuña River, offering pleasant views and rich plant and animal life. Unique geological features like striking gypsum outcrops and historical trenches from the Spanish Civil War can also be observed. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7115885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Risco de las Cuevas of Perales de Tajuña&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7084663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Vaciabotas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38241721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morata de Tajuña Town Square – Tajuña Greenway loop from Tielmes&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 20.6-mile (33.2 km) path through agricultural landscapes and along sections of the Tajuña Greenway. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway – Chapel of the Holy Children loop from Tielmes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Perales De Tajuña?
A: The Tajuña Valley generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. While summer can be warm, the riverside sections provide some shade. It's always advisable to check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the Vía Verde del Tajuña?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns connected by the Vía Verde del Tajuña, such as Perales de Tajuña, Arganda del Rey, Morata de Tajuña, Tielmes, Carabaña, and Ambite. Look for designated parking areas within these towns to access the Greenway.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the Vía Verde del Tajuña?
A: The Vía Verde del Tajuña is generally dog-friendly, allowing well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always good practice to ensure your dog is under control and to clean up after them to maintain the trail for all users.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Perales De Tajuña?
A: The touring cycling routes around Perales De Tajuña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the accessible nature of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, the scenic agricultural landscapes, and the charming towns connected by the routes.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes connect several picturesque towns like Perales de Tajuña, Morata de Tajuña, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón&lt;/a&gt;. These towns offer opportunities to explore local culture, architecture, and gastronomy, including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel and relax during your ride.

Q: Which towns can I explore along the cycling routes?
A: The cycling routes, particularly the Tajuña Greenway, connect several charming towns. You can explore Perales de Tajuña itself, as well as Arganda del Rey, Morata de Tajuña, Tielmes, Carabaña, Ambite, and the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón&lt;/a&gt;, known for its Plaza Mayor.

Q: Do I need any special permits for cycling in the Perales De Tajuña region?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for touring cycling on the Vía Verde del Tajuña or other public routes in the Perales De Tajuña region. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

Take advantage of this bridge over the Vía Verde del Tajuña to rest in the shade, something rare on this route! These bridges were used to cross the old train track that is now converted into the green cycle path.

This small town of about 600 inhabitants has a lot of history behind it. Its name probably comes from the Latin "ambitus" which indicated a fenced place. In the old station there is a perfect bar for a stop on the route.
